sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.61 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: We call a metric space narrow\/ if it is quasiisometric to a subset of the real line, and wide\/ otherwise. Let$G$be a group with the finite set of generators$A$, and let$= (G,A)$be the Cayley graph of$G$with the natural metric. Suppose that, after deleting any narrow subset$L$from$$, at most two connected components of the graph$L$can be wide, and there exists at least one such a subset$L$yieldin...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
